spec_match.rationale: The page beautifully showcases compound logical conditions (and, or, not) combining named-feature() with property-value declarations and selectors.

interactive.rationale: Features an interactive compound query builder where users can input custom expressions and instantly evaluate them in the current browser.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns perfectly with the index description by detailing logical operators and proving combined CSS detection capability.

edge_cases.rationale: A parser issue in evaluateCondition strips outer parentheses from property:value queries, which can cause standard single-argument CSS.supports() calls to return false incorrectly.

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus entry as well as the CSS Conditional Rules Level 5 spec link.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the showcase design guidelines, utilizing a clean layout, interactive buttons, and well-structured contrast states.

openQuestions.title: Fix incorrect parenthesis stripping in CSS.supports calls
openQuestions.detail: In both the KNOWN catalog iteration and the custom evaluateCondition helper, the code strips outer parentheses from strings starting with '(' (e.g. (anchor-name: --x) becomes anchor-name: --x) before passing them to single-argument CSS.supports(). According to the CSS Conditional Rules spec, a single-argument CSS.supports() evaluates a supports-condition, which requires property declarations to be parenthesized. Stripping them causes CSS.supports() to return false. Keep the parentheses intact when invoking single-argument CSS.supports().
